channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Facing a government shutdown deadline, the Senate rushed through final passage early Saturday of a bipartisan plan that would temporarily fund federal operations and disaster aid, dropping President-elect Donald Trump's demands for a debt limit increase into the new year.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: German Christmas market ramming is the latest attack to use vehicles as deadly weapons
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A car-ramming at a Christmas market in Germany, which police are treating as an attack, is the latest in a grim series of events in which vehicles have been used as deadly weapons.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, only the fourth time that’s happened since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.” On eight consecutive nightfalls, Jews gather with family and friends to light one additional candle in the menorah — a multibranched candelabra.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it’s celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WGN 9

item.title: DC violent crime dips 35% in 2024, reaches 30-year low: US Attorney
item.description: Violent crime in Washington, D.C., has seen a 35% decline in 2024 compared to 2023, and violent crime in the district is now at a 30-year low.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time. Each year, at least 100,000 kids call into the North American Aerospace Defense Command to inquire about Santa’s location.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KTLA 5

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report alleges Coast Guard leaders kept sexual assault investigation secret
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former top Coast Guard officials hid a yearslong investigation into sexual assault and harassment at the service's academy from both Congress and the public after leaders debated the fallout from a potential disclosure, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin police chief overseeing school shooting probe is named to Seattle job
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Madison Police Chief Shon Barnes was named as the chief of police in Seattle on Friday, five days after he was thrust into the national spotlight because of a shooting at a religious school in Wisconsin that killed a teacher and student.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER and TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military orders new safety guidelines for key Osprey part after near-crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The military has ordered new safety guidelines for a key part in all Osprey aircraft after a recent accident revealed the same problem that caused a fatal crash in Japan last year. During a Nov.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple delivery hubs. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union launched a strike at seven of the company's delivery hubs less than a week before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA updates definition of 'healthy' on food labels for first time in 30 years
item.description: A new rule from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) will update what it means for food to be labeled “healthy” for the first time in 30 years, a move that aligns with current nutrition science and is meant to help consumers better navigate food labels.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New legislation will provide Social Security benefits to more retirees
item.description: The U.S. Senate is expected to pass legislation to expand Social Security benefits to nearly three million retirees before lawmakers leave for the holiday break.
item.dc:creator: Jessi Turnure, KTLA 5

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' Workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: As the post-pandemic return-to-office push gathers steam, again, workers are quietly mounting a pushback of their own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, ABC 4

item.title: Suspected UnitedHealthcare assassin Luigi Mangione's plan to 'wack' CEO revealed in journal entries: affidavit
item.description: Suspected UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin Luigi Mangione allegedly kept a journal in which he mused over the crime and wrote a letter "To the Feds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Congress strikes deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional leaders have struck a bipartisan deal to keep the government funded beyond a looming Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Tom Cruise awarded highest Navy honor for a civilian
item.description: The 62-year-old actor was celebrated for his outstanding contributions to the Navy and the Marine Corps.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Stock market today: Wall Street trims its stellar gains, Dow slides again
item.description: The Fed is widely expected to announce the third cut of the year to its main interest rate on Wednesday, and officials are also scheduled to unveil projections about where they see rates heading in upcoming years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month. Taco Bell’s Crispy Chicken Nuggets features tortilla breading and will be served with three sauces, including the first-ever Hidden Valley Fire Ranch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9
